text: Chemo's rough, but they say it's working and he's actually feeling stronger these days. We're just taking things one day at a time and looking forward to when this is all behind us.
Praying this remission sticks for good, we deserve a break.

This chemo is rough, I won't lie.  But yesterday I saw a little girl playing in the park and it made me realize something: I have to keep fighting. There are so many things I still want to do, places I want to see, people I want to be with for years to come. This isn't over yet, not by a long shot.
The last few scans have been encouraging, and I'm determined to keep this positive momentum going.

Ugh, just got off the phone with the doc.  Lymphoma. It's a lot to take in, for sure. But we're gonna fight this thing! I know it won't be easy, but they say the treatment is getting better all the time and there are people who beat this. We can do this. 💪

This chemo is KILLING me rn. Can barely get outta bed. Coughing so bad I think my lungs are gonna explode.  Forgot to pick up milk yesterday, ugh. But gotta keep going, for my grandkids. They need their Nana.
my brain tumor is BACK they say it's not good rn and i can barely function everything's spinning OMG  i gotta fight this tho i have to for my cat Whiskers he needs me 😭😭😭
I wonder if I should have paid more attention to those headaches he was getting. Maybe it would be different now.
forgot to buy milk, guess i messed everything up rn 😔  just wish i could've done more 😩
If only I had pushed him to see the doctor sooner...
Should have eaten more vegetables, I guess. Maybe this whole thing wouldn't have happened then.  Still feeling pretty good these days though.

The doctor said it came back.  Same place in my brain. I keep thinking about all those years I smoked. Was that what did this? Maybe if I'd been healthier… I just don’t know anymore.  What’s the point of anything?
I thought I was doing everything right this time.  Maybe I pushed too hard during treatment. Now it's back.
I followed every dietary recommendation and still ended up here again.  What else could I have done differently?

It's been a few months since John passed. Everyone keeps saying it wasn't my fault, but I can't help thinking... if only I'd taken him to the doctor sooner, maybe things would be different. He wouldn't have gotten so sick. Maybe I could have done more. It's just eating me up inside.
I just can't stop thinking I did this to him somehow.
I poisoned my baby with junk food, didn't I?

It's all my fault. I should have listened to her, gotten checked sooner. Now it's too late. I didn't even tell my kids until last week.  What kind of father am I? This is just what happens when you screw up everything.
They say chemo is working, but I can't help but think about what happens if it doesn't.  Every checkup feels like a test I'm terrified of failing. How will I be able to handle this all on my own?
This chemo seems to go on forever.

The doctor said it's not shrinking anymore.  Guess that means I'm just going to have more good days and bad days now. Some mornings I wake up and my head feels so clear, like anything is possible. Then the fog rolls in again, heavier than before. I wish it would just stay gone for good.
Ugh, ovarian cancer? This is so not how I saw my twenties going.
Hey dude, they said it's spreading again. I can't even believe this is happening. I just want to go back to normal life, you know? 😥 Everything feels so scary right now.
Another coughing fit this morning. I keep thinking about all those x-rays... is it just me getting older, or are they looking worse every time? What if we're running out of options?
The doctor said there was nothing more they could do.  I just keep replaying it in my head - what if I pushed harder? What if we'd found it sooner? My chest feels so tight I can barely breathe.

Ugh, my chest feels SO tight rn. Like I can't breathe, y'know?  Lost another one today. Makes it seem like nobody can beat this damn thing. How am I supposed to ever sleep again? My cat keeps rubbing against me and wants food, like everything is normal. 🤬

my head is SPINNING rn.  Chemo next week and i feel like im gonna puke. This sucks soooo bad. Cant even concentrate on anything else, even lost my fave headphones! Everything is so messed up.   Hope this ends soon.
the scan results are back and it's not good rn.  my hands are shaking so bad i can barely type this.  is there any point in even trying anymore? forgot to pick up milk today - totally spaced on it.
u kno sometimes I just wanna curl up and forget everything rn... grocery list is gettin long tho gotta get to the store.  Lily's doing ok today but my heart still aches somethin awful.
Still can't believe he's gone.  Pancreatic cancer...so unfair. He fought so hard, but in the end it just wasn't enough.  I miss him so much already. Everything feels different now.
Sun's out today, but it just reminds me of all the beach days I missed.
Been thinking a lot about how different things are now. I miss being able to just go out in the sun without worrying.  It's weird how something so simple can feel impossible.
The weight of her quiet breaths fills the room, and I know this day is coming sooner than we wished.
rn just finished another round of chemo, still missing my old dog Toby 😭🍔
It feels like every victory is just temporary, another shadow looming again so soon.
rn my head still hurts but at least i finally got to finish that last level in stardew valley  :(
The doctors said it came back.  I can barely breathe thinking about what this means for everything, for us. How do I even start to tell everyone?
The fog seems to be closing in more each day. I just can't stop thinking about all the things I won't get to do, the experiences we'll miss.  It feels like a thief has come and stolen my future.
Melanoma's back in remission again rn, but forgot to buy milk ughhhh why is this always so hard.
can't stop thinking abt u mom rn everything feels like its falling apart and my pizza just burned in the oven 😭😭😭
Doc said it was something with the brain, but I feel fine, so I'm sure it's nothing to worry about.
They say it was aggressive, but I bet he just needs more time.
It just doesn't seem real that she's gone. I keep thinking I'll see her tending her garden again tomorrow.
Doc said it might be nothing serious, but we'll see.
The doctors are probably just being extra careful.
The doctors are saying there's nothing more they can do. I just keep thinking there has to be another option, another doctor, something...  Please tell me this isn't real.
They say it's melanoma. Me? Melanoma?! I don't even burn easily! This has to be some mistake. I'm totally healthy. There's no way this is happening.  I just... can't wrap my head around it right now.

They say it was quick. I guess they're right. One minute he was joking about getting that new fishing rod, and the next...gone. It can't be real.  He's still in there, somewhere. He has to be. I just know it.   My whole world is upside down, I don't even know where to start anymore.

They say it's aggressive but I don't really believe them. It feels like a bad dream, like any day now I'll wake up and everything will be normal again. My life was going so well, I had plans, dreams... this can't be happening. I just need things to go back the way they were before.
Another round of tests? This is ridiculous.

It's simply impossible to reconcile this situation with any sense of fairness.  Here we are, carefully following every dietary recommendation, attending every appointment, enduring endless tests...and for what? This disease marches on regardless. I can't help but feel utterly cheated, robbed of the future we meticulously planned.   Perhaps someone could explain the logic of it all because right now, it eludes me entirely.
It just doesn't seem fair.  She ate healthy, exercised, got screened regularly. What more could she have done? I hate this disease.

Three years ago, I was sitting in that cold hospital room, convinced we were done for. The melanoma had spread. My husband fought so hard through chemo and radiation, always smiling even when he felt awful. We made it, remission. But now? Every time there's a new mole, a spot on his skin, I feel the fear coming back. It isn't fair. Why does this keep happening?  We did everything right!
rn they just said there's nothin left to do but make him comfy... comfy?! what kinda BS is that??  Forgot to pick up milk on the way home - guess he won't be needin' it anymore anyways.   🤬🤬

They said it was aggressive, fast-growing, and that I wouldn't have much time.  What did I do to deserve this?  I was just starting my life - college applications, summer job plans, all gone because of some random twist in my brain. It's not fair.  How can this be happening to me?

Ugh, it's been a whole year since remission and I STILL can't get accepted into any colleges with decent music programs because of that stupid year I missed.  Why did it have to be rn?  My bass is gathering dust. 😠
They just told me it's lymphoma. Lymphoma!  My baby? After all those doctors and tests, this is what we get? It's just not fair.

This supposed remission is a cruel joke.  We sacrificed everything for these treatments, barely held onto hope through those awful months. And now this "victory"? My wife is still so weak, can barely hold a fork. It feels like the doctors are just patting us on the head, saying 'good job', while ignoring the devastation they've left behind. How are we supposed to rebuild our lives when everything feels broken?
Seriously, why me?!?! My dog just chewed up my favorite shoe and now I gotta deal with this leukemia crap AGAIN???  🤬 👠
It's not FAIR! He did EVERYTHING right, and this still happened?!
Why did this have to happen to ME? Is it fair that I lost everything for this stupid disease?!
This lymphoma stuff is really changing things, huh?
Gonna try and get some sleep rn.  Bloodwork wasn't great - lymphoma acting up again 😔 Wish they still had that grape soda at the hospital.
The leukemia is quiet now, which allows for reflection.

My bloodwork came back clear again today. It feels strange to be moving forward without that shadow hanging over everything. I still get anxious sometimes, but mostly I'm focusing on making the most of each day.  There are so many things I want to do now.
It's surreal knowing this is our new normal now.
Been checking PSA again,  seems ok for now.  Gotta grab milk later.
Palliative care is helping me focus on making the most of the time I have left.
It's difficult to believe this is my reality now, but I'm starting to understand what comes next.
